q.en: What did Joseph Montgolfier think made smoke and sparks rise up the chimney?
q.zh: 约瑟夫·蒙戈尔菲耶认为是什么让烟和火星从烟囱里升上去的？

options.en: Wind pushed the smoke up the chimney.
The chimney sucked the smoke upward.
The smoke was lighter than the air around it. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 烟雾比周围的空气轻。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A special gas he called "Montgolfier Gas" made by the burning fire.
options.zh: 他所说的由燃烧产生的“蒙戈尔菲耶气体”。

explain.en: He believed the burning fire created a gas he called "Montgolfier Gas."
explain.zh: 他认为燃烧的火产生了一种他称为“蒙戈尔菲耶气体”的气体。

q.en: In the Montgolfiers' demonstration, which animals were the first aircraft passengers in history?
q.zh: 在蒙戈尔菲耶兄弟的演示中，历史上第一批“飞行器乘客”是哪几种动物？

options.en: A farmer and his son. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一位农夫和他的儿子。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Two French pilots.
A duck, a rooster, and a sheep.
options.zh: 一只鸭子、一只公鸡和一只绵羊。

options.en: A cat and a dog.

explain.en: They attached a basket and sent a duck, a rooster, and a sheep into the air.
explain.zh: 他们挂上篮子，把一只鸭子、一只公鸡和一只绵羊送上了天空。

q.en: In this book, what does the word "dirigible" mean?
q.zh: 在本书中，“dirigible（可操纵航空器）”一词是什么意思？

options.en: Filled with hot air only. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只充满热空气的。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Powered by electricity.
Made of rubber material.
Able to be directed or steered.

explain.en: It means able to be directed or steered, now referring to any steerable lighter-than-air aircraft.
explain.zh: 意思是“可以被操控或转向的”，现在指任何可操控的比空气轻的飞行器。

q.en: Why was the Hindenburg filled with hydrogen instead of helium?
q.zh: 为什么兴登堡号充的是氢气而不是氦气？

options.en: The United States refused to sell helium to Germany.
options.zh: 美国拒绝向德国出售氦气。

options.en: Helium was too heavy to lift the ship.
options.zh: 氦气太重，无法升起飞艇。

options.en: The zeppelin company preferred hydrogen. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 齐柏林公司更喜欢氢气。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Hydrogen was cheaper than helium.

explain.en: The U.S. was the only country with enough helium and refused to sell it to Germany.
explain.zh: 美国是唯一拥有足够氦气的国家，并拒绝出售给德国。

q.en: According to the book, why does a hot air balloon rise?
q.zh: 根据本书，热气球为什么会上升？

options.en: Wind pushes the balloon higher.
Hot air is less dense than the air around it.
options.zh: 热空气比周围的空气密度小。

options.en: The sun pulls the balloon upward.
The balloon is filled with water vapor. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 气球里充满水蒸气。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Heating air spreads its molecules apart, making it less dense than the surrounding air.
explain.zh: 加热使空气分子分散，使其密度小于周围空气，从而上升。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要意图是什么？

options.en: To tell a fictional adventure story. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 讲述一个虚构的冒险故事。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To sell tickets for airship rides.
To inform readers about the history and science of lighter-than-air flight.
options.zh: 向读者介绍比空气轻的飞行器的历史与科学知识。

options.en: To persuade readers to become pilots.

explain.en: The book explains the science and history of balloons and airships to inform readers.
explain.zh: 本书向读者讲解气球和飞艇的科学原理与历史。

q.en: Who made the first powered flight in history with a dirigible?
q.zh: 谁驾驶可操纵航空器完成了历史上第一次动力飞行？

options.en: Henri Giffard, in 1852.
Bertrand Piccard, in 1999.
Count von Zeppelin, in 1900.
Joseph Montgolfier, in 1783.
options.zh: 约瑟夫·蒙戈尔菲耶，于1783年。

explain.en: Henri Giffard flew his steam-powered dirigible 27.4 km in 1852.
explain.zh: 1852年，亨利·吉法尔驾驶蒸汽动力飞艇飞行了27.4公里。

q.en: In what year did Bertrand Piccard and Brian Jones fly a balloon around the world?
q.zh: 伯特兰·皮卡德和布莱恩·琼斯在哪一年乘气球环游了世界？

options.en: 1852. according to some versions of the story

explain.en: They completed the first balloon flight around the world in 1999.
explain.zh: 他们于1999年完成了首次气球环球飞行。

q.en: What is a "zeppelin" as described in the glossary?
q.zh: 词汇表中的“zeppelin（齐柏林飞艇）”指的是什么？

options.en: An airship with a rigid internal framework.
options.zh: 具有刚性内部框架的飞艇。

options.en: A type of hot air balloon. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种热气球。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A steam-powered airplane.
A small weather balloon.

explain.en: It is an airship with a rigid internal framework and cells containing lifting gas.
explain.zh: 它是具有刚性内部框架、并装有升空气体的飞艇。

q.en: What new airship is Germany's Zeppelin Company building today?
q.zh: 如今德国的齐柏林公司正在建造哪种新飞艇？

options.en: The Hybrid Airship only.
The Zeppelin NT (New Technology).
The Goodyear blimp.
The Hindenburg replacement. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 兴登堡号的替代品。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: They build the Zeppelin NT, used for patrol, exploration, advertising, and passenger travel.
explain.zh: 他们建造齐柏林NT，用于巡逻、探索、广告和载客旅行。

prompt.en: Imagine you could design your own lighter-than-air aircraft. What would it look like and what would it be used for?
prompt.zh: 想象一下，你可以设计自己的比空气轻的飞行器。它会是什么样子，用来做什么？

en: Think about whether it would be a balloon, blimp, or zeppelin.
Describe its size, power source, and one special feature.
zh: 想想它是气球、软式飞艇还是齐柏林式飞艇。
描述它的大小、动力来源和一个特别的功能。
